titlestmt.titleproper: Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy collection39-6-2818

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-17 18:17:38 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy collection

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection contains documents, correspondence, and publicity regarding the founding operations administration and projects of the Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy (CRESP)and subsequently the Cornell Center for Transformative Action (CTA).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy collection, #39-6-2818. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 18, folders 15 and 16, restricted to permission of the office of origin.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Center for Religion, Ethics and Social Policy began in 1971 to house the social justice efforts that emerged from Cornell United Religious Work in the 1960's. In 2006, the group adapted to deal with a polarized global society and brought transformative action to the forefront of their efforts and changed their name to the Center for Transformative Action. In 2011 fiscal sponsorship offerings were expanded to include projects from across New York State.

did.unittitle: Subseries Ia. Organization, development, governance, and philosophy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A copy of CRESP's certificate of incorporation, letters, charts, memos, newspaper clippings, and an academic paper on the involvement of Daniel Berrigan in CRESP. Names: John Lee Smith; Tom E. Davis; William W. Rogers; Tom E. Davis; Chandler Morse; Dan Finay; Paul E. Gibbons; Michelle Whitman; Suzanne Grandon; Roger Lazoff; Roger Cramton; Philip Snyder; Joseph A Palnermo; Barry Adams; David Kooperman; Mary Webber; David Connor; Jack Lewis; David Burak

scopecontent.p: Letters

scopecontent.p: Documents regarding CRESP staff, positions, board meeting minutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for a CRESP building in downtown Ithaca are discussed. Includes maps, notes, meeting minutes, letters, real estate listings and plans. Names: Philip Snyder, Sara Hess, Terry Garrison, Michael koplinka-Loehr, Sandy Lyons

scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings, CRESP highlights, articles about CRESP history, purpose, and programs

scopecontent.p: includes an "Annual Report 1978-79"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Khan (Thorp Lecture) 1986, Peter Seeger (sponsored by Cornell Young Democrats) 1986, Sweet Honey In the Rock Concert 1985-1990

did.unittitle: Cornell Coalition for Divestment: Time for Divestment

scopecontent.p: Report, parody preliminary exam, news article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The program aimed to prevent nuclear war between the USA and Soviet Union. Ben and Jerry's Ice-cream was a business collaborator on this project. Folder includes drafts, letters, funding appeals, meeting minutes, proposals, business endorsements, fliers, pamphlets, newspaper clippings Names: Mike Shuman; Neil Schwartzbach; Ben Cohen; Terry Ehrich; Paul Hawken; Philip Snyder; Ira Fishman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: November 11th Committee is the Cornell Chapter of United Campuses to Prevent Nuclear War. Folder contains pamphlet, Newsletter, letters Names: John Lee smith; United States Department of Agriculture; John H. Whitlock; Arthur H. Peterson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This project concerning the New York State College of Agriculture at Cornell University was funded through the Center for Community Change, Washington D.C. Contains academic papers and reports of a study on land grant colleges Names: John Lee smith; United States Department of Agriculture; John H. Whitlock; Arthur H. Peterson; J. Micheal Gatehouse; Nyle C. Brady; Robert A. Plane; donation records; United States Senate; the Assembly State of New York; Jim Hightower; Chandler Morse; Eward H. Smith; David Connor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Journey" was a film by Peter Watkins about the nuclear arms race shown in Cornell Cinema co-sponsored by November 11 Committee, CRESP, and Film/Video Arts. Folder includes pamphlets; letters; notes; news paper articles Names: Richard Herskowitz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CRESP gave this project a grant. Folder contains proposals Names: Tom Danforth; Philip Mallory Jones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: PeaceNet aimed to develop a computer network to connect peace groups Names: Phillip Snyder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Founding Proposal; Annual Reports 1996,2000; Correspondence Names: Paul Gibbons, Lynn Anderson, Anke Wessels

did.unittitle: San Francisco Mime Troupe: "I Ain't Yo' Uncle"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CRESP arranged the San Francisco Mime Troupe to perform at the Statler in Cornell University on February 28th, 1992. Folder contains letters, contracts, publicity fliers, program, photograph Names: Lenore Olmstead; Rena Shagan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ivan Illich was invited to speak in a public lecture at Cornell. Folder contains correspondence and announcements regarding his visit to Cornell, letters, a news release, a copy of Illich's speech Names: Ivan Illich; John Lee Smith

scopecontent.p: Syllabus, evaluations, academic papers Names: John Lee Smith, Stephen Jones
